forecast_7day.short_forecast: Mostly Sunny then Slight Chance Showers And Thunderstorms
forecast_7day.detailed_forecast: A slight chance of showers and thunderstorms after 3pm. Mostly sunny, with a high near 83. North northeast wind around 5 mph. Chance of precipitation is 20%. New rainfall amounts less than a tenth of an inch possible.

forecast_7day.short_forecast: Patchy Fog then Showers And Thunderstorms Likely
forecast_7day.detailed_forecast: Patchy fog before 7am, then showers and thunderstorms likely. Mostly sunny, with a high near 81. Northeast wind around 2 mph. Chance of precipitation is 60%. New rainfall amounts between a quarter and half of an inch possible.

A chance of rain showers between 8am and 11am, then showers and thunderstorms likely between 11am and 3pm, then showers and thunderstorms. Partly sunny, with a high near 80. West northwest wind around 2 mph. Chance of precipitation is 90%.
Showers and thunderstorms likely after 9am. Partly sunny, with a high near 80. Chance of precipitation is 60%.

forecast_7day.short_forecast: Slight Chance Rain Showers then Chance Showers And Thunderstorms
forecast_7day.detailed_forecast: A slight chance of rain showers between 8am and 2pm, then a chance of showers and thunderstorms. Mostly sunny, with a high near 80. Chance of precipitation is 30%.

forecast_7day.short_forecast: Mostly Sunny then Chance Showers And Thunderstorms
forecast_7day.detailed_forecast: A chance of showers and thunderstorms after 2pm. Mostly sunny, with a high near 82. Chance of precipitation is 30%.

forecast_7day.short_forecast: Partly Sunny then Chance Showers And Thunderstorms
forecast_7day.detailed_forecast: A chance of showers and thunderstorms after 2pm. Partly sunny, with a high near 80. Chance of precipitation is 30%.

active_alerts.headline: Air Quality Alert issued July 16 at 10:55AM EDT by NWS Greenville-Spartanburg SC
active_alerts.description:
The North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality in Raleigh
has issued a Code Orange Air Quality Action Day for Ground Level
Ozone for Mecklenburg County, until 8 PM EDT this evening.

An Air Quality Action Day means that Ground Level Ozone
concentrations within the region may approach or exceed unhealthy
standards. For additional information, please visit the North
Carolina Division of Air Quality Web site at
https://airquality.climate.ncsu.edu/.
